# Implementation Plan: Multi-Session Foundation

**Branch**: `051-multi-session-foundation` | **Date**: 2026-05-30 | **Spec**: `specs/051-multi-session-foundation/spec.md`  
**Input**: Feature specification with 10 user stories and 43 functional requirements

**Note**: This plan is generated by the Speckit planning command. See `plan.md` structure below for execution workflow.

## Summary

F-051 addresses the inherited-stale-state problem encountered during F-051's own 2026-05-30 launch. The feature establishes a multi-session foundation for Specrew, enabling multiple developers to work on different features concurrently without merge conflicts on session-state files or state corruption. Cardinality: Carved from Proposals 010 + 134 with a 5-iteration (1, 2a, 2b, 3, 4), ~60.5 SP delivery model within the approved 45-65 SP envelope (honest re-estimate 2026-05-31). Every iteration is capped at ≤20 SP; original Iteration 2 was split into 2a/2b because it packed four user stories over the cap. Iteration 1 (configuration + file classification, ~11 SP) is the dependency gate that unblocks the rest.

**Primary Technical Approach**: Layer 1 multi-developer coordination via session-mode config flag, per-session file classification with gitignore discipline, collision detection via `.specrew/active-sessions.yml`, feature claims tracking via `.squad/active-features.yml`, multi-developer auto-detection, Spec-Kit 0.8.18 upgrade, and brand-new worktree state recognition to eliminate false stale-state recovery prompts.

---

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: PowerShell 5.1+ (Specrew runtime)  
**Primary Dependencies**: PowerShell (Get-Module, Set-Content, Test-Path), Git (author detection, branch analysis), YAML (config + state files), JSON (append-only logs)  
**Storage**: File-based (`.specrew/active-sessions.yml`, `.squad/active-features.yml`, `.squad/identity/session-state.yml`, `.specrew/config.yml`, `.gitignore`)  
**Testing**: Specrew validation framework + acceptance scenario tests per user stories  
**Target Platform**: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, PowerShell 7.x (cross-platform)  
**Project Type**: PowerShell module enhancement (Specrew core tooling)  
**Performance Goals**: Collision detection within 2 seconds, multi-developer signal detection within one `specrew start` command, upgrade completion in under 2 minutes  
**Constraints**: Atomic file writes via write-temp-rename pattern, 24-hour stale lock threshold, session-state local-only (no network transmission), Proposal 148 Layer 2+3 explicitly out of scope  
**Scale/Scope**: Single-developer delivery model, 5 iterations, ~60.5 SP honest re-estimate within the 45-65 SP envelope (Iteration 1 ~11, 2a ~10, 2b ~13, 3 ~13.5, 4 ~13; all ≤20 SP cap)

---

## Phase 1 Quality Planning

**Phase Scope**: `phase-1-first-slice` (F-051 qualification via Iterations 1-4 covering foundation + multi-session coordination)

**Quality Governance Applied**:

- **Maintainability & Testability** (mandatory): Every feature MUST remain reviewable, modular, and covered by acceptance scenarios testing requirement-critical paths. FR-001 through FR-043 include detailed acceptance scenarios.
- **Security & Privacy** (mandatory): The feature touches session state, file permissions, and multi-developer coordination. FR-043 requires machine fingerprinting to remain local-only (no network transmission).

### Stack Surfaces in Scope

| Stack Surface | Path Globs / Evidence | Technology | Why It Matters |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Specrew core (CLI) | `Specrew.psd1`, `Specrew.psm1`, `scripts/` | PowerShell 5.1+ | Session mode configuration, state management, collision detection |
| Configuration layer | `.specrew/config.yml` | YAML | Session mode flag, stale lock threshold |
| State management | `.specrew/active-sessions.yml`, `.squad/active-features.yml`, `.squad/identity/session-state.yml` | YAML + JSON Lines | Session locking, feature claims, per-session state |
| File classification | `.gitignore` generation + git rm --cached | Git + PowerShell | Per-session file isolation, conflict avoidance |
| Spec-Kit integration | `.specify/extensions/specrew-speckit/` | Node.js/Spec-Kit 0.8.18 | Upgrade mechanism and validation |
| Boundary sync | `specrew start`, lifecycle boundaries | PowerShell + Git | Feature claim refresh, multi-developer signal aggregation |

### Risk Dimensions

| Risk Dimension | Status | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Security** | required | Session state includes user/machine identity; atomic writes and local-only fingerprinting are critical controls |
| **Concurrency** | required | Two developers claiming same feature simultaneously requires atomic writes and race-condition handling |
| **State Corruption** | required | Stale lock clearing and invalid YAML recovery are essential for production reliability |
| **Multi-Developer Detection** | required | False positives (single developer, multiple identities) must be advisory, not mandatory |
| **Backward Compatibility** | required | Single-session mode MUST continue to work unchanged; multi-session is opt-in |

### Quality Tool Bundle

| Area | Selection | Evidence / Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bundle ID | `quality-profile.powershell-cli-governance.v1` | Specrew core CLI tooling with mandatory traceability and governance checks |
| Mechanical Checks | Anti-pattern detection (hardcoded paths), test-integrity (acceptance scenarios for all P1 user stories), specification-to-task traceability (every FR → task) | Validator output + test execution logs |
| Ecosystem Tools | `Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer` (PowerShell linting), git history analysis, YAML validation | Free community tools available in Specrew environment |

### Required Quality Gates

| Required Quality Gate | Category | Evidence Source | Phase 1 Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FR traceability check | mechanical | `specs/051-multi-session-foundation/tasks.md` (all FRs → tasks) | planned (Phase 2 task generation) |
| Acceptance scenario coverage | mechanical | User stories 1-10 with explicit acceptance scenarios | completed in spec.md |
| Atomic write validation | manual-evidence | Race condition testing (two developers starting sessions ≤5ms apart) | planned (Iteration 1-4 test execution) |
| Configuration validation | tooling | `.specrew/config.yml` schema + validation on `specrew config set` | planned (Iteration 1) |
| Session state cleanup | tooling | Stale lock detection (≥24h age) + removal on next `specrew start` | planned (Iteration 2a) |
| Multi-developer signal detection | mechanical | Git author counting, machine fingerprint cardinality, branch fan-out pattern detection | planned (Iteration 2b) |
| Identity split enforcement | manual-evidence | Grep for `session_state_` in tracked files (must not find any) | planned (Iteration 4) |
| Brand-new worktree detection | manual-evidence | Fresh worktree launch without stale-state A/B/C prompt | planned (Iteration 4) |

### Not-Applicable Dimensions and Rationale

| Dimension / Gate | Why Not Applicable in This Feature | Follow-up |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Performance profiling (sub-second response) | Session detection happens at human-interactive speed (start of session); 2-second latency is acceptable for initial implementation | Consider perf optimization in future iterations if telemetry shows bottleneck |
| Network resilience | Machine fingerprinting is local-only per FR-043; no network calls in scope for F-051 | Layer 2 (Proposal 148) may introduce network-based claims; evaluate then |
| Internationalization | Specrew error messages and status output are English-only; no i18n in scope | Defer to broader Specrew i18n effort if required |
| Mobile support | PowerShell/Specrew targets desktop/server environments; no mobile in scope | N/A (product constraint) |

### Explicit Phase 2+ Deferrals

- Pre-implementation hardening gate (security review of file write operations, race condition analysis, validation of atomic write patterns, identity split validation) remains deferred to before-implement boundary.
- Dedicated bug-hunter lens execution for file-system race conditions and YAML corruption recovery remains deferred to before-implement hardening phase.
- Quality-drift comparison and reference-implementation checks remain deferred unless explicit F-051-specific known-traps corpus seeding is approved.

## Phase 2 Hardening and Specialist Review Planning

**Phase 2 Slice Scope**: Before-implement hardening gate for all iterations (security review mandatory before any code execution)

**Hardening Gate Artifact**: `specs/051-multi-session-foundation/quality/hardening-gate.md` (to be created before Iteration 1 code begins)

**Known-Traps Corpus Location**: `.specrew/quality/known-traps.md` (reference for trap reapplication)

### Hardening Focus Areas

| Focus Area | Why It Matters in This Slice | Planned Artifact / Evidence | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Security Surface Analysis** | Session state includes user@machine identity + timestamps; atomic writes must prevent races; fingerprinting is local-only | hardening-gate.md: session-state schema + atomic write pattern validation | required |
| **File System Race Conditions** | Two developers claiming same feature within milliseconds must produce atomic, non-corrupt result | hardening-gate.md: race condition test plan + write-temp-rename pattern justification | required |
| **YAML/JSON Corruption Recovery** | Invalid state files must not crash Specrew or block `specrew start` recovery | hardening-gate.md: corruption detection + empty-file fallback behavior | required |
| **Privacy & Machine Fingerprinting** | FR-043 requires fingerprints to remain local-only; no telemetry or network transmission | hardening-gate.md: fingerprinting scope + network boundary validation | required |
| **Stale Lock Clearing** | 24-hour threshold must be justified; automatic clearing must not lose data for genuine multi-day features | hardening-gate.md: stale-lock clearing logic + edge case handling | required |
| **Test-Integrity Targets** | Acceptance scenarios for US1-US10 must exercise requirement-critical paths (configuration, collision detection, claims, auto-detection, worktree detection) | test-plan.md + acceptance scenario test harness | required |

### Lens Activation Plan

| Lens / Checklist Ref | Activation | Why Activated or Omitted | Planned Evidence / Artifact Path |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Security issues (file writes, fingerprinting) | required | Local file operations + multi-developer state management require security hardening review | hardening-gate.md + security-specialist review |
| Concurrency & race conditions (atomic writes, collision detection) | required | Multi-developer simultaneous claims require provable atomicity | hardening-gate.md + race-condition test plan |
| State machine correctness (session lifecycle, lock cleanup) | required | Session entry creation, heartbeat, cleanup, and stale-lock clearing form a critical state machine | hardening-gate.md + state-machine validation |
| YAML/JSON parsing robustness | required | Invalid state files must fail safely (not crash, not corrupt data) | hardening-gate.md + error-handling validation |

### Routing Policy

| Lens Scope | Requested Reasoning / Review Class | Effective Class (when run) | Override / Approval Record | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Security surface + atomic writes | Strongest available (security specialist) | Effective class TBD at review time | None yet (requires approval before Phase 1 code) | Security Specialist from delegation guidance |
| Concurrency & race conditions | Strongest available (architect) | Effective class TBD at review time | None yet (requires approval before Phase 1 code) | Software Architecture expertise (dial: 10) |
| File-system corruption recovery | Architect + implementer | Effective class TBD at review time | None yet (requires approval before Phase 1 code) | Critical for reliability in multi-worktree scenarios |

### Explicit Later Deferrals

- Full line-by-line hardening gate execution and runtime-only final proof remain deferred until the before-implement boundary formally authorizes Phase 1 code execution.
- Strongest-class routing enforcement details and requested-versus-effective execution evidence remain deferred until the routed lens execution path exists.
- Quality-drift comparison against known-traps corpus and reference-implementation checks remain deferred unless explicit F-051-specific trap seeding is approved.

## Constitution Check

**Status**: PASS with rationale below

- **Spec Authority Gate** ✓: Plan scope maps directly to approved spec artifacts (spec.md defining 10 user stories, 43 FRs, 8 success criteria). No authority violations.
- **Layering Gate** ✓: Changes classified as Spec Kit layer (PowerShell module core + configuration schema) with explicit rationale. No Squad-layer or team-configuration changes in F-051 scope.
- **Traceability Gate** ✓: Each planned deliverable (per-iteration output below) links to specific user stories (US1-US10) and functional requirements (FR-001 through FR-043). Task-level traceability deferred to Phase 2 task generation.
- **Ownership Gate** ✓: Single-developer delivery model (Alon Fliess, current user). Role ownership for implementation, review, and retrospective explicitly assigned to Implementer, Reviewer, Retro-Facilitator roles per `.squad/team.md`.
- **Capacity Gate** ✓: Effort unit = Story Points (SP); iteration capacity = ≤20 SP per iteration per TG-005 (honest re-estimate: Iteration 1 ~11, 2a ~10, 2b ~13, 3 ~13.5, 4 ~13; total ~60.5 SP within the 45-65 envelope).
- **Drift/Reconciliation Gate** ✓: Drift detection strategy = compare implemented features against FR-001 through FR-043; scope drift = detection if implementation adds features not in FRs or omits scoped items. Reconciliation path = Spec Steward reviews diffs, escalates to human if violations found.
- **Verification Gate** ✓: Process verification = Spec Steward drift checks at plan/task/review boundaries; outcome verification = acceptance scenario tests for all P1 user stories (US1-US10) executed during Iterations 1-4; final approval by Reviewer role.

## Iteration Structure & Capacity Planning

**Total Scope**: ~60.5 SP across 5 iterations (honest re-estimate 2026-05-31, within the 45-65 SP envelope; every iteration ≤20 SP per TG-005). Original Iteration 2 split into 2a/2b — see iterations/001/capacity-reestimate.md.

### Iteration 1: Session Mode Configuration & File Classification (Target: ≤20 SP)

**Scope**: FR-001 through FR-006 (6 functional requirements, 2 user stories: US1, US2)

**Deliverables**:

1. `session_mode` config flag in `.specrew/config.yml` with `specrew config set session_mode` CLI command (FR-001, FR-002, FR-003)
2. File classification schema + `.gitignore` generation logic (FR-004, FR-005)
3. Per-session file removal from git index via `git rm --cached` (FR-006)

**User Stories Covered**: US1 (Configure Multi-Session Mode), US2 (Avoid Per-Session File Conflicts)

**Quality Gates**:

- `specrew config set session_mode multi` persists configuration correctly
- `.gitignore` excludes all per-session file patterns (`.specrew/last-*`, `.specify/feature.json`, `.specrew/start-context.json`, etc.)
- Previously tracked per-session files are removed from index without deletion

**Dependencies**: None (foundation iteration)

**Unblocks**: Iterations 2-4 (collision detection and auto-detection depend on session_mode being configurable)

**Estimated SP**: ~11 SP (honest re-estimate: config logic ≈2 SP, file classification ≈3.5 SP, gitignore + git-rm-cached ≈2.5 SP, tests + validation ≈3 SP)

---

### Iteration 2a: Collision Detection & Feature Claims (Target: ≤20 SP)

**Scope**: FR-007 through FR-016 (10 functional requirements, 2 user stories: US3, US4)

**Deliverables**:

1. `.specrew/active-sessions.yml` lock file management (create, heartbeat, cleanup, stale detection) (FR-007 through FR-011)
2. `.squad/active-features.yml` claim tracking + refresh at lifecycle boundaries (FR-012 through FR-016)

**User Stories Covered**: US3 (Detect Concurrent Session Collisions), US4 (Claim Features)

**Quality Gates**:

- Concurrent `specrew start` on same feature within 5 minutes triggers collision warning
- Feature claim recorded at specify boundary, refreshed at each subsequent boundary

**Dependencies**: Iteration 1 (session_mode must be configurable first)

**Unblocks**: Iteration 2b, Iteration 4 (identity split validation depends on feature claims)

**Estimated SP**: ~10 SP (active sessions ≈4 SP, claims ≈3.5 SP, tests ≈2.5 SP)

---

### Iteration 2b: Conflict Reduction & Multi-Developer Auto-Detection (Target: ≤20 SP)

**Scope**: FR-017 through FR-024 (8 functional requirements, 2 user stories: US5, US6)

**Deliverables**:

1. Per-iteration decisions split + JSON Lines append-only logs + Specrew.psd1 FileList alphabetic sort (FR-017, FR-018, FR-019)
2. Multi-developer auto-detection signals (git authors, machine fingerprints, concurrent writes, branch fan-out) (FR-020)
3. Recommendation surfaces (Welcome Orientation, `specrew where` dashboard, boundary-sync output) (FR-021, FR-022, FR-023, FR-024)

**User Stories Covered**: US5 (Reduce Shared-File Merge Conflicts), US6 (Detect Multi-Developer Activity)

**Quality Gates**:

- Multi-developer signals detected and recommendations shown within one `specrew start`
- Merge conflicts on `.squad/decisions.md` and `Specrew.psd1` eliminated via splitting + sorting
- Recommendation suppressed when `session_mode` is already `multi`

**Dependencies**: Iteration 2a (session/claim primitives)

**Unblocks**: Iteration 3 (infrastructure work can proceed in parallel)

**Estimated SP**: ~13 SP (anti-conflict tactics ≈3.5 SP, auto-detection ≈4.5 SP, recommendation surfaces ≈2 SP, tests ≈3 SP)

---

### Iteration 3: Spec-Kit Upgrade & specrew update Fix (Target: ≤20 SP; ~13.5 SP)

**Scope**: FR-025 through FR-034 (10 functional requirements, 2 user stories: US7, US8)

**Deliverables**:

1. Spec-Kit 0.8.13 → 0.8.18 upgrade mechanism (detection, execution, preservation, validation) (FR-025 through FR-030)
2. `specrew update` command bug fix: install version detection + `.specrew/config.yml` version field write (FR-031, FR-032, FR-034)
3. Version drift warning during `specrew start` (FR-033)
4. `--dry-run` support for `specrew update` (FR-034)

**User Stories Covered**: US7 (Upgrade Spec-Kit to 0.8.18), US8 (Fix Baseline Version Bump)

**Quality Gates**:

- Spec-Kit upgrade from 0.8.13 to 0.8.18 completes in <2 minutes
- All governance validators pass after upgrade
- `.specrew/config.yml` `specrew_version` field matches installed module version
- Version drift warning displays when installed version ≠ configured version

**Dependencies**: Iteration 1 (session_mode config must exist for upgrade-time configuration)

**Unblocks**: Iteration 4 can proceed in parallel (independence scope)

**Estimated SP**: ~13.5 SP (upgrade detection ≈3 SP, upgrade execution ≈4 SP, version fix ≈2.5 SP, dry-run ≈0.5 SP, tests + validation ≈3.5 SP)

---

### Iteration 4: Identity Split & Brand-New Worktree Detection (Target: ≤20 SP; ~13 SP)

**Scope**: FR-035 through FR-043 (9 functional requirements, 2 user stories: US9, US10)

**Deliverables**:

1. Split `.squad/identity/now.md` (shared content: focus_area, body; session-state content: per-session fields) (FR-035, FR-036, FR-037)
2. Migration logic to move existing session_state_* fields from now.md to new `.squad/identity/session-state.yml` (FR-037)
3. Validation that tracked files do NOT contain session_state_* fields (FR-038)
4. Brand-new worktree detection heuristics (empty active-sessions, no boundary commits on current branch, no iteration directories) (FR-039)
5. Skip stale-state A/B/C prompt when brand-new detected; preserve prompt when genuinely inconsistent (FR-040, FR-041)
6. Logging of brand-new detection signals and decisions (FR-042)
7. Machine fingerprinting scope validation (local-only, no network transmission) (FR-043)

**User Stories Covered**: US9 (Split Session-State Transient Fields), US10 (Detect Brand-New Worktrees)

**Quality Gates**:

- `.squad/identity/now.md` contains only shared content (focus_area, body)
- Per-session state in `.squad/identity/session-state.yml` remains gitignored
- Fresh worktree launch skips stale-state recovery prompt
- Worktree with prior feature state still triggers recovery prompt when mismatch detected
- Machine fingerprints remain local-only (no telemetry transmission)

**Dependencies**: Iteration 2a (feature claims must exist for identity split integration); Iteration 1 (session_mode and file classification must be in place)

**Unblocks**: feature closure (final iteration)

**Estimated SP**: ~13 SP (identity split ≈2.5 SP, migration logic ≈1 SP, brand-new detection ≈3.5 SP, fingerprinting validation ≈0.5 SP, tests + validation ≈3 SP, docs reconcile ≈2.5 SP)

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/051-multi-session-foundation/
├── spec.md                      # Feature specification (input)
├── plan.md                      # This file (Phase 0-2 planning output)
├── research.md                  # Phase 0 research (if needed for unknowns)
├── data-model.md                # Phase 1 data model & entities
├── quickstart.md                # Phase 1 quick-start guide for F-051
├── contracts/                   # Phase 1 interface contracts (if applicable)
└── tasks.md                     # Phase 2+ task definitions (generated by /speckit.tasks)
```

### Source Code (Specrew Core)

```text
Specrew.psd1                     # PowerShell module manifest (updated with session mode + file classification)
Specrew.psm1                     # PowerShell module implementation
scripts/
├── specrew-cli.ps1              # CLI entry points (config, start, where, update)
├── session-management.ps1       # Active session lock file + heartbeat logic
├── feature-claims.ps1           # Feature claim tracking + refresh
├── auto-detection.ps1           # Multi-developer signal detection
├── file-classification.ps1      # Per-session file classification + gitignore generation
├── spec-kit-upgrade.ps1         # Spec-Kit upgrade mechanism
├── version-management.ps1       # Specrew version detection + update fix
├── state-validation.ps1         # YAML/JSON parsing + corruption recovery
├── identity-split.ps1           # Session-state split logic + migration
└── worktree-detection.ps1       # Brand-new worktree detection heuristics

.specify/
├── extensions/specrew-speckit/  # Spec-Kit 0.8.18 (upgraded from 0.8.13)
└── config.yml                   # File classification schema + session_mode flag definition

.specrew/
├── config.yml                   # Session mode flag (single|multi), version pins
├── active-sessions.yml          # Session lock file (YAML)
└── (gitignored) last-*.*, start-context.json, host-history.json, etc.

.squad/
├── active-features.yml          # Feature claims tracking (YAML)
├── identity/
│   ├── now.md                   # Shared identity content (focus_area, body)
│   └── (gitignored) session-state.yml  # Per-session state (new split)
└── decisions/
    └── iteration-NNN/           # Per-iteration decision files (F-051 Iteration 1-4)
```

**Structure Decision**: F-051 is a Specrew core enhancement (PowerShell module). Changes are scoped to Specrew module manifest + implementation scripts (session management, feature claims, file classification), configuration schema (session mode), state files (active sessions, feature claims, identity split), and Spec-Kit upgrade mechanism. No separate application layer; all changes are in the Specrew runtime.

---

## Risk & Mitigation Summary

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Race condition (two developers claim same feature at same millisecond) | High | Atomic writes via write-temp-rename pattern; both claims recorded, conflict detected at next refresh |
| YAML/JSON file corruption (invalid state files crash Specrew) | High | Validation + fallback to empty/default state on parse failure; logging via FR-042 |
| Stale lock false positives (clearing too aggressively causes data loss) | Medium | 24-hour threshold (configurable via `.specrew/config.yml`); justification in spec assumptions |
| Multi-developer signal false positives (single developer, multiple identities) | Medium | Recommendation is advisory (not mandatory); developer can ignore or configure multi-session mode |
| Brand-new detection heuristic gaps (misclassifying genuinely inconsistent state) | Medium | Explicit heuristics + logging (FR-042); manual testing with various inherited-state combinations in Iteration 4 |
| Spec-Kit upgrade failure (network, permissions, version incompatibility) | Medium | Clear error messages + rollback instructions; pre-upgrade validation; validate post-upgrade with governance checks |
| File classification gaps (missing per-session file patterns) | Low | Comprehensive pattern list in FR-005; migration to catch existing tracked files with `git rm --cached` |
| Version drift detection (installed vs. configured mismatch) | Low | Warning during `specrew start` (FR-033); user can run `specrew update` to reconcile |

---

## Success Criteria & Validation

All success criteria from spec.md section "Success Criteria" apply:

- **SC-001**: Two developers, different features, no merge conflicts on per-session files (100% success rate)
- **SC-002**: Collision warning within 2 seconds when two developers start same feature within 5 min (100%)
- **SC-003**: Multi-developer detection within one `specrew start` after 2nd developer commits (100% for defined signals)
- **SC-004**: Spec-Kit upgrade 0.8.13 → 0.8.18 in <2 min, all validators pass (100% in supported scenarios)
- **SC-005**: `specrew update` version sync accuracy 100% (zero mismatches after running)
- **SC-006**: Merge conflict elimination on `.squad/decisions.md` + `Specrew.psd1` (100% clean merges)
- **SC-007**: Multi-session recommendation shown within 0-2 seconds after signals detected
- **SC-008**: Feature claim refresh at all lifecycle boundaries (100% update rate)

**Validation Plan**:

- Iteration 1: Configuration persistence + gitignore generation (automated tests)
- Iteration 2: Collision detection race test + claim refresh across boundaries (manual two-developer test)
- Iteration 3: Upgrade execution + version sync (automated + manual upgrade on test machine)
- Iteration 4: Brand-new detection on fresh worktree + identity split validation (manual test with new worktrees)

---

## Known Unknowns & Research Needs

**None identified**: Specification is materially clear across all dimensions (confirmed by clarify-phase completion). All technical details (file formats, detection heuristics, upgrade mechanisms, atomicity patterns) are defined in spec.md. No blocking ambiguities.

**Optional future research** (not blocking F-051 plan):

- Performance optimization if telemetry shows session-detection latency is real bottleneck
- Network-based claims mechanism for Proposal 148 Layer 2 (future iteration)
